And the percentage of engaged sessions are, well, let me define engaged sessions first.
So engaged sessions are visits that either lasted longer than 10 seconds or had one page scroll or someone clicked a link on your site.
So for example, if someone visits your homepage, they click to, I don't know, go to a solutions page, that counts as an engaged session.
So usually you will have fewer engaged sessions than actual sessions.
And this is also a good way to measure your bounce rate because the engagement rate is actually the opposite of the bounce rate.
So this, the data.
